headline: KOSPI CRASHES 9.99% to 8,203.84 Tuesday — TWO circuit breakers in ONE session; Samsung -12.3%, SK Hynix -12.5%, Kioxia -15%+; foreign investors dump KRW 5.79tr (~$3.8B) of shares in single-day reversal of Monday's HBM-leadership crowning
summary: South Korea's KOSPI plunged 9.99% to close at 8,203.84 Tuesday, down 910.71 points, triggering a market-wide circuit breaker that halted trading for 20 minutes — the KOSDAQ separately tripped its own halt. Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) fell ~12.3% and SK Hynix (000660.KS) ~12.5%; Kioxia (285A.T) tumbled 15%+ in sympathy. Foreign investors dumped KRW 5.79 trillion (~$3.8B) of Korean shares in a single session — the largest one-day outflow in the rally's history. Catalyst stack: (1) Monday's $250B Alphabet selloff on AI-talent flight (Shazeer to OpenAI, Jumper to Anthropic) triggered de-risking of the AI-memory cohort, since Google is a confirmed HBM3E/HBM4 customer; (2) KOSPI was +90%+ YTD and roughly TRIPLED over the past 12 months — leveraged retail liquidation (Korea margin debt at record KRW 37.7T) accelerated the downdraft; (3) the same Monday crowning of SK Hynix as Korea's #1 by mcap proved to be a textbook climactic top — by Tuesday's close Samsung had retaken the top spot. Read-through to House coverage: (1) MASSIVE negative read into MU's Wednesday June 24 FQ3 print — the asymmetric Stifel/UBS/Deutsche/Wedbush $1,200-$1,500 PT scramble now has rate-of-change risk if Korea-correlation forces a multi-day flush; (2) US semis traded sympathy lower Monday with MU leading the Nasdaq decline; (3) the breadth-crack signal from June 19 Daishin's 100-advancers/810-decliners print which DID NOT extend Monday HAS NOW extended — bear case for AI-memory supercycle gets its first credible test.

headline: Alphabet (GOOGL) loses ~$250B mcap Monday — WORST single-day decline in over a year — on TWO 72-hour AI-talent defections: Noam Shazeer (Transformer co-author, Gemini co-lead) to OpenAI; John Jumper (AlphaFold, 2024 Nobel Chemistry) to Anthropic
summary: Within 72 hours over the weekend, two foundational AI scientists announced exits from Google. June 18: Noam Shazeer — co-author of the seminal 'Attention Is All You Need' paper that birthed the Transformer architecture, and co-lead of the Gemini project — announced he is joining OpenAI. June 19: John Jumper — founder/lead of the AlphaFold project at DeepMind and 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry laureate — confirmed he is leaving for Anthropic after nearly nine years. Context: Google paid ~$2.7B in August 2024 to bring Shazeer back from Character.AI through a tech-licensing deal. Alphabet shares fell ~6% Monday wiping out roughly $250B in market cap — the worst single session in over a year — and continued -2% pre-market Tuesday. Compounded by Monday's separate $75M Google/A24 film-studio AI partnership headline read as creative-distraction. Read-through to House coverage: (1) THIS WAS THE CATALYST that triggered Tuesday's Korea AI-memory crash — Google is a confirmed HBM3E/HBM4 customer and the talent-flight read raises questions about the durability of GOOGL's TPU/AI buildout cadence; (2) structurally positive for OpenAI/Anthropic IPO valuation paths (OpenAI confidential S-1 at $852B was filed June 9); (3) yellow flag for the 'integrated hyperscaler AI moat' thesis that underpins MSFT/AMZN/META valuations.

headline: Oracle (ORCL) DISCLOSES 21,000 LAYOFFS (~13% of workforce) for FY26 in regulatory filing — $1.84B severance cost vs $374M prior year; AI-driven restructuring follows June 11 capex/RPO shock; stock -2% pre-market on top of Monday weakness
summary: Oracle disclosed in a regulatory filing Monday after-hours that it has eliminated approximately 21,000 jobs over the past fiscal year — roughly 13% of its global workforce. Headcount: ~141,000 as of May 31, 2026, down from ~162,000 a year earlier. Restructuring cost: $1.84B in severance, office closures, contract terminations, and relocation, vs $374M in the prior fiscal year. Driver, per company language: 'deployment of AI technologies across our operations has already resulted in workforce reductions and could lead to further job cuts in the future.' Stock reaction: -2% pre-market Tuesday, on top of cumulative ~13% weakness post the June 11 $70B FY27 capex / $20B capital raise shock. Read-through to House coverage: (1) ORCL is now a clear margin-discipline story heading into FY27 — the capex-shock-plus-severance-cost combination tests whether OCI's $638B RPO can translate to GAAP earnings power; (2) supports the broader thesis that legacy enterprise software headcounts are being compressed by AI (compare: Accenture downgrade Monday, NDX CTSH deletion); (3) read-through for SaaS labor base — CRM, ADBE, NOW peers will be measured on similar AI-led headcount actions; (4) modestly POSITIVE for AI infra layer (CRWV, NVDA, AMD) — capital that was going to people is going to silicon.

headline: JPMorgan UPGRADES IBM to Overweight from Neutral Tuesday — PT to $291 from $270 (15% upside) on H2 2026 software acceleration, Red Hat/OpenShift AI-container tailwind, HashiCorp automation reacceleration; stock +4% pre-market
summary: JPMorgan's Brian Essex upgraded International Business Machines (IBM) to Overweight from Neutral Tuesday morning, raising the price target to $291 from $270 (implying ~15% upside to Monday's close). Thesis: greater confidence in a second-half 2026 software acceleration. Specifically called out: (1) Red Hat / OpenShift migration tailwinds as AI-driven container adoption scales; (2) HashiCorp automation reacceleration on growing C-suite sponsorship; (3) software now ~45% of IBM revenue and ~two-thirds of consolidated profit, room for multiple expansion as IBM gains traction as an 'AI infrastructure beneficiary.' Stock +4% pre-market. Read-through to House coverage: (1) IBM not in the AI-Datacenter-Power coverage universe directly, but the upgrade is a cohort signal — the sell-side IS willing to upgrade enterprise software AI-infra winners (rather than only momentum-trading them) even on a Korea-crash session, providing a relative-value floor for ORCL/CRM/SNOW; (2) HashiCorp call-out is positive read for NOW (ServiceNow) AI agent narrative; (3) Red Hat / OpenShift momentum confirms the container-orchestration layer as a structural AI-infra winner.

headline: Qualcomm (QCOM) in ADVANCED talks for $4B acquisition of Modular Inc. — second hyperscale AI-infra play in two weeks alongside Tenstorrent $10B talks; stock -6% pre-market on dilution / strategy-pivot concerns
summary: Per Bloomberg Monday evening, Qualcomm is in advanced talks to acquire Modular Inc. — an AI-infrastructure software company led by Chris Lattner (creator of Apple's Swift programming language and ex-Tesla Autopilot exec) — in a deal valuing Modular at approximately $4B. Modular was last valued at ~$1.6B nine months ago — roughly 2.5x markup. Final agreement not guaranteed; details may change. This is Qualcomm's SECOND major AI-infrastructure M&A move within a fortnight: the company is separately in advanced talks to acquire AI chipmaker Tenstorrent for up to $10B. Stock reaction: QCOM -6% pre-market on the dilution-overhang read. Read-through to House coverage: (1) QCOM is not in the AI-Datacenter-Power coverage universe but this is a STRUCTURAL TAM-EXPANSION SIGNAL for AI-infra software (validating Databricks, Weights & Biases, Together AI, Fireworks AI valuation multiples); (2) negative read for AMD/NVDA on cumulative AI accelerator competition — QCOM is now stitching together a stack (Modular MAX inference + Tenstorrent silicon) targeting NVDA's CUDA moat directly; (3) Chris Lattner's track record (Swift, MLIR, LLVM) signals serious technical conviction behind the deal.

headline: Susquehanna HIKES TSMC (TSM) PT to $575 from $500 — Mehdi Hosseini cites leading-edge capacity FULLY SPOKEN FOR through 2027, AMD/Google/Tesla diversifying to Samsung foundry, Apple-Intel domestic carve-out from TSM — TSM ADRs hit $477.82 intraday Monday
summary: Susquehanna raised its Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) price target to $575 from $500 Monday, citing the now-public reality that TSMC's leading-edge capacity is fully spoken for through 2027. Counter-vector: Apple has agreed to partner with Intel to manufacture chips domestically to reduce TSMC dependence; Google, AMD, and Tesla are diversifying advanced-node orders to Samsung Foundry. TSM's U.S.-listed ADRs (NYSE:TSM) climbed 1.5% to $468.85 Monday, hitting an intraday high of $477.82. Read-through to House coverage: (1) confirms the structural foundry-utilization upcycle — capex-pull-through to AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/ASML/8035.T continues; (2) the multi-foundry diversification narrative is structurally bullish for 005930.KS (Samsung Foundry profitability targeted 2028) and INTC (Apple 18A foundry agreement); (3) modestly NEGATIVE for the single-supplier-risk premium on TSM — but the new $575 PT implies enough capacity discipline that absolute price absorbs it; (4) sympathetically positive for ASML (EUV monopoly) and AMAT/LRCX/KLAC into Q2 prints.

headline: FedEx (FDX) Q4 FY26 prints TODAY after-close — Street expects $5.95 EPS / $24.03B revenue; FIRST report as STAND-ALONE PARCEL company post-Freight spin-off June 1; FY27 guide is the watched catalyst for industrial-cyclical read into AI-buildout logistics
summary: FedEx reports fiscal Q4 2026 results after Tuesday's close — its first quarterly print as a stand-alone parcel/express company following the June 1 spin-off of FedEx Freight. Consensus: $5.95 EPS (+8.15% YoY) on $24.03B revenue; sequential improvement from March Q3's $5.25 EPS. Watched metrics: (1) segment operating margins across Express, Ground, and Freight; (2) volume/yield trends; (3) Network 2.0 cost-program progress; (4) FY27 guidance — first full year post-separation. Read-through to House coverage: (1) FDX is not directly in the AI-Datacenter-Power coverage universe but is the single best high-frequency US industrial-logistics read for inferring datacenter-buildout pace and electrical-equipment shipments; (2) a weak Express/Ground guide would signal e-commerce softness contradicting hyperscaler capex cadence — yellow flag for VRT/ETN/GLW commercial momentum; (3) a strong Express guide combined with margin-expansion print would confirm continued AI-capex pull-through to industrial logistics, sympathy-positive for the GEV/ETN/VRT cohort; (4) commentary on jet fuel costs is a useful real-time Iran/Hormuz read.

headline: Micron (MU) Q3 FY26 print Wednesday June 24 AC — consensus $19.95 EPS / $34.66B revenue (top of $32.75-34.25B guide range); gross margin watched at 81% guide vs Street 81.6% — the cycle-cresting bear case needs sub-80%; 27 analysts Strong Buy, ZERO Sell heading into print
summary: Micron Technology reports fiscal Q3 2026 results Wednesday June 24 after the close. Consensus: $19.95 EPS / $34.66B revenue (LSEG: $35.59B — above the top end of management's $32.75-34.25B guide range). Gross margin is THE watched metric: consensus 81.6%, broadly consistent with management's 81% guide — would be the highest in company history if achieved. HBM commentary on Vera Rubin Ultra 2027 allocations is the most critical forward-looking signal. Coverage: 27 analysts Strong Buy consensus, ZERO Sell ratings entering the print. Recent PT cycle ahead of print: UBS $1,500 (Monday), Stifel $1,500, Deutsche Bank $1,500, Wedbush $1,300, Citi $1,200, Rosenblatt $1,200, Aletheia Capital $1,600 — Goldman the lone bear at ~$400. Read-through to House coverage: (1) ALREADY-rich setup made more fragile by Tuesday's KOSPI -9.99% — Korea-correlation drag means MU enters the print with broken multi-day momentum tape rather than the prior 'inevitable' bid; (2) cycle-cresting bears need sub-80% gross margin to win — that's now the asymmetric event-risk; (3) FY27 capex commentary will be the key on/off switch for AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/ASML/8035.T memory-WFE TAM; (4) any HBM4 ASP commentary frames the 005930.KS / 000660.KS rebound trade.
